original: Ham the chimpanzee becomes the first primate in space, traveling 158 miles aboard Mercury-Redstone 2

1st automobile to exceed 100 mph (161 kph), British daredevil Arthur Macdonald drive a Napier six-cylinder racing car named 'Samson' on sands of Daytona Beach, Florida, notching a top speed of 104 mph
Millions of birds fly over western San Francisco, darkening the sky
Apollo 14 launched, 1st landing in lunar highlands
US launches their 1st artificial satellite, Explorer 1 - leads to the discovery of Earth's radiation belt by James van Allen and his team at Iowa University
"Final Fantasy VII" is released by Square and Sony Computer Entertainment to PlayStation
1st McDonalds in the Soviet Union opens in Moscow
Astronomer Alvan Graham Clark makes first observation of Sirius B (first known white dwarf star) in Cambridgeport, Massachusetts while testing his new telescope
Colonization of the America's in the late 1500s killed so many people it cooled the planet and led to a "Little Ice Age", according to a scientific report published in "Quaternary Science Reviews"
Congress passes, by a vote of 121-24, the 13th Amendment to the US Constitution, abolishing slavery in the United States except as punishment for a crime
Strongest instrumentally recorded earthquake then recorded at 8.8 on the Richter Scale, occurs off the coast of Ecuador and Colombia killing over 1,00 people
RCA demonstrates 1st music synthesizer
10 Arabian oryx (extinct except in zoos) released in Oman
Samuel Gravely assumes command of destroyer escort "USS Falgout", first African American to command a combat ship
Eva Hart, British Titanic passenger, one of last living survivors, born in Ilford, Essex, England (d. 1996)
Scotch tape 1st marketed by 3-M Company
17-year-oldLydia Koof New Zealand becomes the youngest golfer in men's or women's golf history to be ranked No. 1 in the world
Gaetan Boucher skates world record 1000m (1:13.39)
Houston guard James Harden becomes first player in NBA history to log a 60-point triple-double with 60 points, 10 rebounds, 11 assists, 4 steals and a block in Rockets' 114-107 win over Orlando Magic
Philip Glass, American minimalist composer (Einstein On The Beach; Koyaanisqatsi), born in Baltimore, Maryland
KC [Harry Wayne Casey], American disco and funk singer-songwriter and producer (KC & the Sunshine Band - "That's the Way (I Like It)"; "Get Down Tonight"), born in Opa-locka, Florida
Jesse Jamesgang robs a train at Gads Hill, Missouri
French astronomerCharles Messieradds M57 (Ring Nebula in Lyra) to his catalog
Rounding of Cape Horn for the first time by Dutch expedition of Jacques Le Maire and Willem Corneliszoon Schouten (they name Cape Hoorn after Schouten’s hometown)
1st black Civil War regiment, South Carolina Volunteers, mustered into US army
USSR launches Luna 9 towards Moon
US launches HEOS A-2 for interplanetary observations (396/244,998)
American pilot Robert Gibson flies record 27,040 feet (8,242 m) altitude in horizontal flight, over Houston, Texas (since superseded)
Quebec Bulldogs centerJoe Malonesets NHL record with 7 goals in a 10-6 win against Toronto St. Patricks at the Quebec Arena
Aretha Franklinsings at gospel singer Mahalia Jackson's funeral at the Greater Salem Baptist Church in Chicago, Illinois
The Pompidou Centre, designed by architects Renzo Piano and Richard Rogers, housing the greatest collection of modern and contemporary art in Europe, is inaugurated in Paris
Atlanta Hawks set a new NBA monthly win record as they move to 17-0 for January, with a 91-85 win against the Philadelphia 76ers
Super Bowl XXVII, Rose Bowl, Pasadena, CA: Dallas Cowboys beat Buffalo Bills, 52-17; MVP: Troy Aikman, Dallas, QB
Super Bowl XXII, Jack Murphy Stadium, San Diego, CA: Washington Redskins beat Denver Broncos, 42-10; MVP: Doug Williams, Washington, QB
Super Bowl XXXIII, Pro Player Stadium, Miami, FL: Denver Broncos beat Atlanta Falcons, 34-19; MVP:John Elway, Denver, QB
USAF launches Samos spy satellite to replace U-2 flights
Nauru (formerly Pleasant Island) declares independence from Australia the UK and New Zealand, becoming the world's smallest republic
Tony Sheridan& Beat Brothers (later known as The Beatles) record "What'd I Say" & "Ruby Baby"
STS 89 (Endeavour 12) lands
Pope Francislands in Kinshasa on a visit to the Democratic Republic of Congo, first papal visit to the country since 1985
Barcelona opera theater "Gran Teatro del Liceo", built in 1847, burns down for the second time; re-built and re-opens in 1999
In his 13th title defenseJoe LouisKOs Red Burman in the 5th round at Madison Square Garden, New York to retain NYSAC heavyweight boxing crown
West Indian cricket batsman Rohan Kanhai scores back-to-back centuries in the drawn 4th Test against Australia in Adelaide; follows 1st innings 117 with 115 in 2nd knock
Montreal Canadiens beat Hamilton Tigers, 5-4 at the Mount Royal Arena, Montreal; first penalty-free game played in NHL history
S. Newman Darby, American inventor of the sailboard, born in Wilkes-Barre, Pennsylvania (d. 2016)
Christopher Chataway, British MP/athlete (world record 5k), born in Chelsea, London (d. 2014)
Benjamin Hooks, American lawyer, Baptist minister, and civil rights leader (NAACP executive director, 1977-92), born in Memphis, Tennessee (d. 2010)
Bob Turner, Canadian Hockey HOF defenseman (Stanley Cup 1956, 57, 58, 59, 60 Montreal Canadiens; 6 x NHL All Star), born in Regina, Saskatchewan (d. 2005)
Brenda Hale, Baroness Hale of Richmond, British judge, 1st woman to head UK Supreme Court, born in Leeds, England
Carol Channing, American actress (Gentlemen Prefer Blondes; Hello Dolly), singer and comedian, born in Seattle, Washington (d. 2019)
Terry Kath, American rock guitarist, songwriter, and vocalist (Chicago - "Make Me Smile"), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1978)
Jan van Speyk, Dutch naval hero (blew up own gunboat to avoid capture during Belgian Revolution), born in Amsterdam (d. 1831)
Theodore W. Richards, American chemist (1st American to win the Nobel Prize for Chemistry for atomic weights 1914, awarded 1915), born in Germantown, Pennsylvania (d. 1915)
Irving Langmuir, American chemist and engineer (Nobel Prize for Chemistry 1932, invented gas-filled incandescent lamp), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 1957)
Gouverneur Morris, American lawmaker, diplomat and US Founding Father who drafted final version of the US Constitution, born in The Bronx, Province of New York (d. 1816)[1]
Freya Stark, English explorer and travel writer (The Southern Gates of Arabia), born in Paris, France (d. 1993)
Emanuel Sayles, American jazz banjo player (Preservation Hall Jazz Band), born in Donaldsonville, Louisiana (d. 1986)
Atahualpa Yupanqui, Argentine folk musician, born in Pergamino, Argentina (d. 1992)
Alan Lomax, American ethnomusicologist, and folklorist (Library of Congress Archive of American Folk Song), born in Austin, Texas (d. 2002)
E. Fay Jones, American architect (Thorncrown Chapel), born in Pine Bluff, Arkansas (d. 2004)
